houses (counted of course from either the Sun or Lagna) transit-
ing the various Bhavas from either. Now 1 shall give the details
of the usual Gocara system. Taking the natal Moon as the

246 FUNDAMENTALS OF ASTROLOGY
starting point you have to proceed to consider which planets are
good or bad and in which houses. In this case too you must
be exact and scientific in your method. For this purpose you
must make a new Bhava chart with the longitude of the Moon at
birth as the Lagna. If this is done properly, you can say whether
a planet in transit is in one or an other house from the Moon.
For example, let us suppose that the Moon is in the 29th degree
of Mesa. If the Sun in transit conies to the 4th degree of
Vrsabha. you should not think that the Sun is in the 2nd house
from the Moon. He should be considered to be in the first
house itself. The Sun produces good effects when he passes in
